full_prompt: Design the structural shell of an ARLISS 2025 Open-class CanSat. Per the ARLISS 2025 introduction document, the Open class allows a cylindrical envelope of diameter <= 146 mm and mass <= 1050 g, deployed at ~3.7 km AGL from a commercial high-power rocket and required to survive both the launch load (peak ~20 g axial during motor burn) and the landing touchdown. The shell dimensions are: outer diameter 146.0 mm (with -0.2 mm tolerance to fit the fairing ID), overall height 300 mm, wall thickness to be designed. Two interface features are required: (a) an upper mounting ring of 8 mm thickness with six M3 through-holes on a 120 mm bolt circle to interface with the recovery parachute bridle and internal bulkhead; (b) a lower skid plate of 10 mm thickness bonded to the base, providing a landing impact surface of 120 mm diameter. Material for the shell is woven CFRP [0/90]s laminate with equivalent Ex = 55 GPa, Ey = 55 GPa, Gxy = 4 GPa, nu_xy = 0.05, density 1550 kg/m^3, in-plane tensile allowable 600 MPa, compressive 550 MPa, interlaminar shear 55 MPa. Landing skid is 6061-T6 aluminium. Load cases: LC1 (Launch axial) — 20 g axial compressive acceleration on the full 1050 g CanSat mass distributed along the shell length, with the upper ring bolted to a simulated bulkhead as the load reaction; LC2 (Launch lateral) — 5 g lateral acceleration with base fixed; LC3 (Deployment pyro shock) — 500 g lateral shock over 1 ms at the upper ring (worst-case separation event); LC4 (Landing impact) — 20 m/s vertical terminal velocity (representative of failed parachute deployment) against a rigid ground; LC5 (Nominal landing) — 10 m/s terminal velocity against a rigid ground (nominal parachute descent). Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1, composite shell peak stress < 400 MPa (tensile allowable / 1.5) and no local buckling (linear eigenvalue factor >= 3.0); (R2) under LC2, max von Mises anywhere < 400 MPa; (R3) under LC3, peak stress < 300 MPa (allowing a Miner's-rule transient margin); (R4) under LC4, max deflection of shell inward < 5 mm and no structural failure at 1.5x safety factor; (R5) under LC5, no plastic deformation of skid plate or shell, max von Mises in skid < 184 MPa; (R6) total shell + skid + ring mass < 350 g so internal payload has 700 g margin; (R7) outer diameter under LC1 must not exceed 146.2 mm (fairing ID clearance). Primary verification: linear static structural FEA for LC1, LC2, LC3; linear buckling eigenvalue for LC1; explicit dynamic FEA (LS-DYNA / Abaqus Explicit) for LC4, LC5. Thermal, fluid, vibration modal, and radiation analyses are not required for this benchmark. Output one STEP AP242 assembly plus a report listing total shell mass, max stress per case per material, buckling factor under LC1, inward deflection under LC4, and OD change under LC1.
short_prompt:
Design the structural shell of an ARLISS 2025 Open-class CanSat. Open class allows a cylindrical envelope <= 146 mm OD and <= 1050 g wet, deployed from a commercial HPR at ~3.7 km AGL.

Envelope: 146.0 mm OD x 300 mm tall, wall to be sized; 8 mm-thick upper ring with 6 x M3 on a 120 mm bolt circle (bridle + bulkhead); 10 mm-thick 6061-T6 lower skid plate of 120 mm diameter bonded to the base; woven CFRP [0/90]s shell.

Factor regime: FoS 1.5 on composite tensile allowable for linear cases; buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0 under axial launch; deflection and residual-envelope gates under explicit impact cases; fairing-ID clearance preserves OD <= 146.2 mm.

Load cases.
- Launch axial 20 g distributed along the shell with the upper ring treated as bulkhead-bolted.
- Launch lateral 5 g with base fixed.
- Deployment pyro shock: 500 g over 1 ms at the upper ring (separation).
- Failed-chute landing at 20 m/s onto rigid ground.
- Nominal-chute landing at 10 m/s onto rigid ground.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Landing impacts (LC4, LC5) require explicit dynamic solution.

prompt.title: ARLISS 2025 Open CanSat shell
prompt.brief: CFRP cylindrical shell Ø146 x 300 mm with aluminium skid, sized to ARLISS Open class 1050 g cap, surviving 20 g launch and failed-chute 20 m/s impact.

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Descent rate prescribed by parachute sizing, not body CFD.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Vibration qualification to Orion motor sweep
